text.title: Logstash Pipeline Configuration and Tuning Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules grounded in Logstash docs + GDPR/PCI compliance + production incident postmortems.

rule.statement: PII MUST be masked in the filter stage BEFORE the Elasticsearch output. Masking post-index is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Once indexed, PII may be cached, backed up, replicated; recall is incomplete. Masking before index is the only compliant path.

rule.statement: Heavy processing (grok, useragent) MUST live in a separate pipeline from lightweight routing. Single-pipeline mixed workloads are forbidden in prod.
rule.rationale: Heavy filters block worker threads; mixing with routing causes head-of-line blocking across all streams.

rule.statement: Dead-letter queue MUST be enabled (`dead_letter_queue.enable: true`). Dropping unparseable events is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without DLQ, parse failures vanish silently; debugging is impossible. DLQ captures failed events for replay after fix.

rule.statement: `pipeline.workers` MUST be set to physical core count (or 1.5x for I/O bound). `pipeline.batch.size` MUST be tuned (typical 125-500).
rule.rationale: Default 1 worker = single-thread bottleneck. Untuned batch size starves the bulk API.

rule.statement: Every grok / json filter MUST set `tag_on_failure` so failed events are routable to DLQ. Silent failure is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without `tag_on_failure`, failed events still reach output (without parsed fields) — they look 'successful' but are corrupt.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
